title: Vegan Trip to Botswana was Magical!
text: It was an amazing once in a lifetime experience, that I got to experience twice.  I visited on my own in May of 2025, and just loved every moment with the animals, that I decided to return in January of 2026 with my new fiancee.  The first time I tried the mobile safari with 3 nights at Deception Valley Lodge.  The mobile safari allowed us to get very close to the animals in their own habitat.  But the lodging experience was a bit rough.  So the second time we decided to treat ourselves to luxury and only stay at Lodges.  It was 3 nights at Moremi and 3 nights  back at Deception.  Helene was super helpful in making sure everything was vegan during the mobile safari.  She also coordinated with the lodges to make sure they only served me vegan food.  I really enjoyed the camaraderie of traveling with my fellow vegans.  The staff was absolutely amazing.  They really cater to your needs and make a point to get to know you.  Botswana is magical and is mostly undiscovered.  As opposed to safaris in Kenya and Tanzania, or places like Bali, that have been overrun by tourism.

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana
placeInfo.address: Exit 5 Disaneng, Maun, Ngamiland East P.O.Box 319 Botswana

title: An incredible experience!
text:
We were allowed to experience a 5-day safari (2 nights at Lazy River Camp and 2 nights at Moremi Game Reserve) with Shangana safaris at the end of October/beginning of November, it was an incredible experience! 
Already the booking was very professional, Helene was always contactable by email and responded quickly. 
The safari itself was super organized. Our guide master was incredibly good and could tell us a lot about the animals, plants and the land. Also the other employees:inside did a great job without exception and were always concerned about our well-being.  
For us as vegans:inside was special the very delicious food with great vegan alternatives. 
All in all, the safari far exceeded our expectations and is an experience we will not forget in our lives. Thank you Shangana safaris!

title: Best safari ever
text: A truly wonderful experience! Botswana is itself an astonishing place, but with the right people's expertise, like Joseph and his teammates, and the flawless organization right in to the heart of the wilderness, the journey can become perfect. The cherry on top has been having Helene, the manager, spending 3 days with us in the bush, that added her knowledge and charm to the whole. I can't recommend them enough, and i wish I'll be back one day. ❤️

title: Honeymoon Safari
text:
My wife and I went on an 8 night honeymoon safari in Botswana with Shangana Safaris, spending two nights in the Okavango Delta, three nights in Moremi and then three nights in Khwai. We camped rather than staying in lodges. 

As we are both vegan, we were unsure about our safari options when first looking around, and Shangana Safaris offer a vegan safari package which was our main reason for booking with them. 

The food on our safari was incredible and you would never realise it had been prepared and cooked in a camp site rather than a restaurant kitchen. We had an amazing range of different meals each day and were never left hungry. 

Our guide, Joseph, was both an incredibly friendly and welcoming person as well as a very knowledgeable guide who cares deeply about the conservation work within the game reserves. His expert experience meant he was able to spot animals from far away and follow tracks and animal patterns, to ensure that we had amazing sightings of so many different animals which we would never have found by ourselves. 

We had some last minute flight time changes coming into Maun, my wife’s luggage was lost by the airline and I experienced some motion sickness while in the camp when it was too late for me to find a shop to buy tablets in. Helene and David worked around the flight changes for us, helped to track down our luggage and arrange for it to be transported to the camp, and purchased motion sickness tablets to be brought down for me. 

We really felt that everyone involved with our safari went out of their way to help with any issues we had and to make our stay as comfortable and enjoyable as possible. We are both so thankful to everyone for making our honeymoon so special and memorable for us.

Botswana as a country has the most amazing game reserves and wildlife. 

We can’t recommend Shangana Safaris enough!

title: Two phenomenal safari adventures with an experienced company.
text:
We went on two separate safaris with this company with a group of friends. The first one was 7 days and we went to the Moremi game reserve and the Khwai community concession area. Both were full of wildlife and birds. The guides were so knowledgeable about all aspects of the area, including knowing the birds, which is a special interest of ours. They also were very proud of Botswana and knew and shared many details of this wonderful country. We traveled with 12 people and had two guides with safari vehicles, four staff and one chef for support. The guides were very respectful of the wildlife and the area, and really worked hard to enable us to see a real variety of wildlife. The camp staff made our tent camping very comfortable and the food was plentiful and delicious. 
      Our second safari was also 7 days and we went to the Makgadigadi pans park and Central Kalahari park. This time there were on six of us, one guide, two camp staff and one chef.  the same comments for above, go here as well. The guide was so accommodating to our interests and we saw so many new animals and birds. He was so patient stopping when we asked as well as when he saw something of interest. He really know the flora as well, which is so integral to understanding the challenging conditions of this area. Again, the camping was comfortable and food plentiful and delicious. 
     I would highly recommend this company.

title: Super safari with Shangana!
text:
I just got back from a six-day mobile safari.
Perfect organization.
Our guide, Master, was exceptional and allowed us to see lions, cheetahs, leopards and all the other animals present in the Okavango Delta! All really close up.
Exceptional staff. The cook cooked different foods every day. All delicious.
The tents are simple, but comfortable. Really convenient to have your own bathroom, in a connecting tent.
The cold of the night is bearable, if well covered: I recommend also using a thermal sweater and wool socks.

title: Fantastic experience with Shangana Safaris
text:
We worked with Helene from Shangana Safaris and couldn't be happier with the safari experience she arranged for us.  The trip included safari camps in Botswana and Kenya, plus a stopover in Victoria Falls, with multiple bush plane flights and ground transfers.  All the logistics worked seamlessly which allowed us to enjoy the experience.  We essentially asked for door to door service, which we've never done before while traveling, and Helene delivered.

On our arrival in Maun at the start of the trip, Helene graciously met us at the airport and drove us to our lodging at Maun Studios, which we found to be charming.  The staff were friendly and helpful, even driving us back and forth to a local restaurant that evening since we didn't rent a car.  From Maun, we flew to Leroo La Tau, Xakanaxa, and Chobe camps in Botswana, followed by a stopover in Victoria Falls at the 528 Guesthouse, and then to Kenya where we stayed at the Karen Gables Guesthouse in Nairobi before completing our itinerary at Ol Pejeta and Entim Mara bush camps.  Without exception, all camps were excellent with top notch service and friendly, knowledgeable guides.  The lodgings in Maun, Victoria Falls, and Nairobi were also excellent and we recommend each place highly.  The food was great, with only a minor quibble that vegetarian options at some lodges were somewhat limited.  But staff members bent over backwards to accommodate us, whether it involved the food, daily activities, or other logistics.  We highly recommend all the establishments above.

Helene also arranged specific excursions for us, including a hot air balloon ride in Kenya and a visit to the the northern white rhino sanctuary in the Ol Pajeta Conservancy.

All the bush plane flights were fun and added to the experience by providing an interesting view of the varied landscapes, plus wildlife spotting opportunities.  We took a helicopter ride over Victoria Falls which was somewhat expensive for the short ride, but we would still recommend it.  Our trip was in May and the falls were thundering with huge plumes of spray which was beautiful.  The staff at 528 Guesthouse were very helpful, arranging the helicopter tour as well as an evening dinner cruise.

All this added up to a fantastic safari experience that exceeded our expectations.  Whether it was the big cats, elephants, crocodiles, giraffes, zebras, wildebeasts, jackals, hyenas, ostriches, rhinos, hippos, beautiful birds, or the many other animals we were fortunate to see, it was the experience of a lifetime and we're already thinking about future trips to Africa.  We enthusiastically recommend Helene and Shangara Safaris.

title: Vegan safari
text:
I just returned from a 6 night camping safari with Vegan Safari Africa/Shangana Safaris. Our guide Joseph was extremely knowledgeable, kind and a wonderful host. He truly cared for us to have a great experience and went out of his way to make sure to spot and track animals and get us as close to them as possible while still remaining safe. The rest of the staff were welcoming and accommodating and the tent accommodations were comfortable. I loved the camping experience as it felt like we were really experiencing the African bush. The vegan food was fresh, delicious and plentiful.

This safari was the best experience of my life and highly recommend this company to other travellers looking for a vegan friendly safari.

title: Incredible Mobile Camping Experience in Botswana
text: We went mobile camping as part of our trip through Shangana Safaris and had the most incredible experience.  We spent three nights at Moremi, three at Khwai, and two at Savuti with the same staff.  Our guide, Master, was phenomenal, as compared to all the other guides we saw on our three week trip and consider him a friend (as well as the other staff).  We asked about animal behavior, local culture, bird sounds, animal identification, the environment, plants, etc. and he knew everything.  He could spot animals at an instant and could track prints to find the big predators.  We had a large cabin tent, cots with mattresses, rugs inside, hot water morning and night, and a private latrine and shower area attached to the back of the tent.  The staff for the two of us consisted of our guide Master, a chef, and two helpers that set up camp and took good care of us.  The owners, David and Helene, supplied great food, snacks, and drinks.  We would have a morning drive starting in the dark, a coffee/tea/snacks break at a nice location (table, chairs, table cloth) mid-morning, and were back at camp for lunch.  The afternoon drive started at 4:00 and then we would set up at a nice animal viewing location to watch the sunset with snacks/drinks.  We would arrive back at camp after dark, have dinner, sit around a campfire, and start it all over again the next morning. This type of camping allows you to camp in the parks so you do not waste travel time, hear the animals at night, smell the wild sage and basil permeating the air, experience a night drive at one park, and avoid many of the crowds you see at Chobe.  We highly recommend Shangana Safaris!

We asked about animal behavior, local culture, bird sounds, animal identification, the environment, plants, etc. and he knew everything.  He could spot animals at an instant and could track prints to find the big predators.  We had a large cabin tent, cots with mattresses, rugs inside, hot water morning and night, and a private latrine and shower area attached to the back of the tent.  The staff for the two of us consisted of our guide Master, a chef, and two helpers that set up camp and took good care of us.  The owners, David and Helene, supplied great food, snacks, and drinks.  We would have a morning drive starting in the dark, a coffee/tea/snacks break at a nice location (table, chairs, table cloth) mid-morning, and were back at camp for lunch.  The afternoon drive started at 4:00 and then we would set up at a nice animal viewing location to watch the sunset with snacks/drinks.  We would arrive back at camp after dark, have dinner, sit around a campfire, and start it all over again the next morning. This type of camping allows you to camp in the parks so you do not waste travel time, hear the animals at night, smell the wild sage and basil permeating the air, experience a night drive at one park, and avoid many of the crowds you see at Chobe.  We highly recommend Shangana Safaris!

title: Exceptional  A CKGR Safari I Loved it!
text:
I’ve lived in Maun for over 30 years and finally treated myself to a  mobile safari. I joined a friend from Australia who was keen to see the Central Kalahari, and we booked with Shangana Safaris. This was a good  decision.
Effortless and genuine. Locally-run outfit knows what they’re doing, everything just worked, with no fuss.
We saw large herds of oryx and springbok, and had the luxury of enjoying it all without crowds.
Comfort in the bush. Warm beds, spotless bush ensuite, and little extras when you least expected making for a fantastic experience ,surprises like homemade yogurt, fresh breads, desserts, and wine by the fire. 
A fantastic crew. Master (guide), Moffet (camp hand), and MG (chef) made us feel welcome, safe, and thoroughly looked after.
It was relaxed, real, and I believe good value.

title: The most memorable and beautiful experience ever
text:
Helene and her team really looked after us and paid attention to every detail to make sure our trip was unforgettable. 

From the calls in advance to go over everything and understand all of our wishes for the trip, then meeting us at the airport and introducing us to our most wonderful guide Joseph, then of course the adventure began and we went on the experience of a lifetime! 

We were taken on daily tours of the bush, served great vegan meals every day, went on a dugout canoe ride, tried basket weaving and camped in the middle of the Moremi game reserve for multiple nights where we got to see these incredible animals living in their natural environment. 

We saw lions, elephants, wild dogs, giraffes, zebras, buffalo, wildebeest and more! Joseph is incredibly kind and knowledgable and was always teaching us things about the wildlife. It's hard to put into words, but these memories will stay with our family forever. 

Thank you Helene, David, Joseph and team!

title: SAFARI INOUBLIABLE
text:
We returned from a fabulous 10-day family trip on mobile safari organized by Shangana safari. Everything was perfect!
Helen and David even picked up our luggage which arrived 2 days later and sent it to us on the camp. My husband had a medicine in his luggage, they managed to find it! To go with them is to leave quietly, they can manage everything!!
We had fabulous moments, evenings by the fire listening to animals: unforgettable. Thanks to our Mazda Guide and his knowledge of animals, we witnessed incredible scenes of life. MJ and his teammates Mulla and Obi (sorry for the spelling) were very caring with us: excellent dishes cooked on a wood fire, hot showers every day, campfires morning and evening.
10 exceptional days: we congratulate ourselves for trusting Shangana safari.
Thank you Helen and David for this unforgettable trip!!

text: Just got back from the most amazing safari hosted by wonderful people at Shangana Safaris. I cannot recommend this company enough. Thank you Helene at Shangana you provided one of the best ever times of my life.

title: Look no further you have found your next Botswana Safari!
text: Shangana Safaris created a trip of a lifetime for my wife and I! We worked with Helene who helped us create a 13 day trip to Botswana that included a stay at Maun Studios in Maun upon arrival. From there we did Shagana's 8 night Maun to Kasane vegan mobile safari. Followed by 3 nights at the Bakwena Lodge in Kasane. The whole trip was amazing and Helene's communication throughout the entire booking process was great. The Shangana / Vegan Safari Africa 8 night vegan safari was definitely the highlight of the trip. During that portion of the trip we were accompanied by only one other guest from California and then three team members from Shangana. The Shangana team during the mobile safari included Joseph as our guide, MG was the cook,  and Obi who assisted MG and set up camp. Joseph was very knowledgeable and a great guide. He really made the most out of everyday, and by the end of the trip we truly felt spoiled on how well he was able to track animals as well as share so much information about the conservation efforts Botswana has performed since their independence. We were also amazed on how delicious the food was during the camping.  MG’s culinary skills were amazing and we were surprised on how every meal was different. It even gave us a few ideas to try at home.  And we can’t forget Obi! He was very nice and was on top of any request we had while we were at camp. We are so glad we chose to go with Shangana Safaris and would choose them again if we ever make it back out to Botswana (which we hope we do!). Shangana's passion for the environment and conservation, communication, and affordability are a just a few of the many great qualities that should make them your next choice for a Botswana safari.

title: Wonderful mixed safari experience in Botswana with Shangana Safaris
text: My wife and I had an amazing safari experience in Botswana, all expertly and seamlessly arranged by Helene at Shangana Safaris. We did a mix of mobile camping in Moremi, fancier lodge at Deception Valley, classic/rustic camp at Oddballs, and a guesthouse in Victoria Falls. It was the "shoulder" season (late March/early April) and we had the parks and camps to ourselves. We enjoyed the variety of open Landcruisers, walking through the floodplains, and flying low between sites.  All of our guides and staff were first rate. The vegetarian food was excellent and plentiful - we did not starve!  The air transfers between camps were timely and quick.  It was easy to focus on being with the many animals and each other.  Bravo and well done to Shangana for a wonderful experience all around!

title: The 3 S's 》Splendid, Superb & Spectacular
text: Splendid properties, superb service & spectacular game drives were our experience  each day of our safari thanks to Helene's expertise & personal touch in booking & making all arrangements. She synthesized numerous emails from my friend & I into a magical itinerary that suited both our tastes & preferences without either of us having to settle & created the trip of a lifetime for us. She truly paid attention to our desires & made them a reality. We were always looked out for every step of the journey traversing 6 lodges/camps in 4 countries over 2 weeks & every regional flight, boat ride & vehicle transport was expecting us & ready to keep the thrills coming & joy flowing. Whether it's your first trip to Africa like my friend's or fourth visit to the continent like mine you won't find a better planner or have a better experience with anyone else or another company.

title: Amazing vegan safari!
text:
We had an amazing safari experience! Helene arranged a fantastic tour for us with our mobile tented safari. We felt like the locations were well chosen and each gave us a different perspective.  She also arranged our pre and post stays. The Maun
studios, Chobe Bakwena Lodge and 528 Guesthouse were all wonderful. Good thing we had an overnight in Maun studios presafari as the airline lost a suitcase. On
our mobile safari, all of the staff were friendly, diligent and
 professional.  We appreciated the fact that Shangana does not use radios in their safari vehicles.  The radios invariably result in safari becoming an artificial Disneyworld/spectator sport- mobs of safari vehicles surrounding each sighting.  Without the radio,  the guides can really use their skills and we have a true adventure each day with sense of real Africa  Special commendation to our guide Master whose expert tracking
skills and knowledge of the animals placed us in the right places at
 the right time to have some wonderful experiences alone with the
 wildlife without the safari paparazzi.  This included being surrounded by hundreds of elephants crossing the river and hanging out with wild dogs during their afternoon siesta.  My favorite was when we came across a crowd of safari vehicles watching a leopard hidden in some grass eating her kill. He asked us if we wanted to join the queue as night was falling. We decided that we did. But after our turn when all the other vehicles moved on, he suggested we linger a bit longer.  We got to see a National Geographic special unfold -as a hyena followed the scent to the leopard who raced with her dinner up a tree.  The hyena waited patiently for the bone scraps that eventually fell then left.  The leopard came down again for a final cleanup. So many magical moments.  All the tourists rushing off to the next photo op missed it. As for camp-the tents and cots were comfortable. The food was excellent.  I loved that we had local dishes like pumpkin stew. I was especially impressed with the gourmet vegan cheeses-even better than what is available in the US! I could eat those cheeses with that crispbread everyday! Our Botswana safari was a fantastic adventure. Compared to my earlier safari in Tanzania, the Botswana safari was a far more authentic experience. We got to be fully immersed in the African ecosystems.  It was amazing to hear elephants and lions walking through camp at night while we were safe in our tents. A honeybadger even broke into our toilet tent to get the water in the basin.  How cool is that? This was a REAL safari !

title: Amazing experience
text: My wife, our eldest daughter and I spent a week in Moremi and Khwai with David and Helene's incredibly proficient team.  Apart from living inside a Nat Geo magazine for the week, the guides and support crew made the week for us.  Nothing was too difficult for them and their knowledge of the environment and wildlife was fascinating and very educational.  Camp locations, camp facilities and vehicles were all excellent - as was the food - catering for all dietry needs - nothing overly flash but very wholesome and more than satisfying!  We'd love to return to Bots (well, Maun specifically) and spend more time exploring an amazing part of our planet.  Thanks Helene, David and team for a wonderful experience.  PS After Moremi we spent another 10 days kayaking on the Okavango with David's brother, John.  That was an equally rewarding experience!  Hence the urge to return!

title: Experience the wild
text:
Truly immersive experience in nature's wonderland-Okavango! Each day was filled with fascinating encounters with animals in the wild, observing their behaviour amongst themselves and interacting/communicating with other species.The incoming waters at  the Delta was a veritable Noahs Ark- attracting the entire animal kingdom! The big 5 - in the first morning.
Highlights-witnessing a leopard cub dragging an impala up a tree, wild African dogs feeding 14 pups at their den, two male lions marking their territory around our camp
Shangana Safaris' Helene was professional , always available and organised a very safe and enjoyable experience for our family - parents and twin girls
Our camp guides were extremely well informed, camp catering was generous and of a high standard

title: Good value!  Good sightings, recommend it!
text:
We  (a family of 3 with a teenager) did the 4D3N Delta + Moremi tour with 2 nights at Lazy river and 1 night at Moremi.
David (owner)/Helene  did our booking. Both were very responsive. 

The Lazy river camp site was next to a quiet and beautiful lagoon. The shared toilet/shower onsite were clean.  The bucket shower worked well, the water was warm enough.  Elephants and hippos were nearby. We could hear them. 

Moremi portion: We saw 3-4 sightings of lions and one with 4 cubs (pretty close to them!).  We were happy with the sightings as  we were only there  for  a day and half We didn't see the leopard - time was a little short.   Other animals were spotted (giraffes, elephants, red leches, ostrich, jackals, even a honey badger!)  The camp staff and guide were helpful in general.  This was our last stop for our  3-week long trip. If we had more time, the adults would have stayed for 2 more days.  But my teenage kid had enough game drives ...(she had more than 20+ game drives already by the time we got to Moremi).

A suggestion: maybe do the mokoro trip at Ditshiping station if time allows. We had free time to go a little further on the mokoro trip day.

title: Moremi day safari
text:
This was the most amazing experience……… a day safari they said… we didn’t expect what we got at all……. 
The ride out  to Moremi was beautiful… we stopped for hot drinks and beautiful rusks, then we were off…… 
We had an amazing guide Joseph who stopped at nothing to track the animals. We were so lucky to see a plentiful of giraffe, elephant, hippo zebra, ostrich, impala, and literally hundreds of marabou storks…… when we thought it couldn’t get any better we found 3 beautiful lionesses resting under shrubs, we had the pleasure of taking in their magnificent. 
Then as we turned to leave we found 5 small cubs with another lioness that had been watching us. Just amazing…… 
Just for the record our lunch was 5* , it was like a bistro meal. A laid table, with an abundance of beautiful homemade foods. 
We can’t recommend this company enough, 
Thank you
